title: Allowing Deserialization of LDAP Objects is Security-Sensitive
description: JNDI supports the deserialization of objects from LDAP directories, which can lead to remote code execution. This rule raises an issue when an LDAP search query is executed with SearchControls configured to allow deserialization.

checks.message: Forbidden method 'ldapContextInit' or 'directoryContextInit' called, which can lead to LDAP deserialization.
Public methods should not allow LDAP deserialization. Consider making this method 'private' or 'protected'.
Static methods should not allow LDAP deserialization. Consider removing the 'static' modifier.
Method 'search' should not be configured to allow deserialization.
SearchControls should be provided when executing LDAP search queries.
SEARCH_SCOPE_SUBTREE should be used instead of SEARCH_SCOPE_ONELEVEL or SEARCH_SCOPE_BASE when executing LDAP search queries.
SEARCH_CONTROL_RETURN_OBJECT should be used instead of SEARCH_CONTROL_DONT_RETURN_OBJECT when executing LDAP search queries.
